ksa_text: Package and deploy machine learning models to production serving infrastructure including containerization, API wrapping, load testing, and rollback procedures.

ddat.top_element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply chain issues.

top_anchors.element_label: Capability building for digital, data and technology

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Technical Foundations falls outside the primary competency scope of the VP Data Governance. This task KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Technical Foundations truly out-of-scope for the VP Data Governance? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Technical Foundations as a core or recommended competency for the VP Data Governance. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Technical Foundations falls outside the established competency profile for the VP Data Governance. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Technical Foundations is outside the competency scope of the VP Data Governance. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
